Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. LDL cholesterol is mildly elevated. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or renal disease.
